Nitrogen-fixing plants are those that have a symbiotic relationship with bacteria in their root nodules, converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form. Legumes like beans (2), peas (4), and lentils (5) are well-known nitrogen fixers. While some other plants can fix nitrogen, these are the most common examples in this context.

Bioreck technology, also known as bioconcrete or bio-cementation, involves using microorganisms to precipitate calcium carbonate, effectively binding materials together. This process is particularly useful for developing building materials from plant residues, creating a more sustainable and eco-friendly construction option.
